# Operational Python attenuated-backscatter validation — benchmark stations

*Generated by `validation/paper/run_paper_validation.py`. The calibration (Rayleigh from L2_monthly + cloud from L2_daily), Kalman smoothing, water-vapour and wavelength corrections, screening, gridding and statistics are all the operational **Python** routines (no MATLAB). Statistics are over 500-3000 m AGL vs the reference channel; the MATLAB columns are the previous `R_*.mat` results for the same stations.*

> **Rayleigh** channels reproduce the MATLAB to the profile (N) and correlation (r); the relative-bias offsets are calibration-value differences (window-median Kalman vs the MATLAB full-archive recompute), not pipeline differences. **Cloud** channels apply the O'Connor coefficient as `beta_true = (C · 1e6) · attbsc_0` — the 1e6 restores the physical 1/(m·sr) scale the cloud reader integrates on (the L2 attbsc_0 is stored in Mm⁻¹sr⁻¹). The result is physically scaled and the same sign/order as MATLAB (e.g. CL31 +60% vs +32%); the residual ~1.5-2× is the Python-vs-MATLAB cloud-method value difference, with matching r.

| station | channel | calib | Python relbias | Python r | Python N | MATLAB relbias | MATLAB r | MATLAB N |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| payerne | CHM15k (Rayleigh) *(ref)* | rayleigh | +0.0% | 1.000 | 79431 | +0.0% | 1.000 | 79431 |
| payerne | CL31 (cloud) | cloud | +60.1% | 0.434 | 79348 | +31.8% | 0.477 | 79348 |
| payerne | CL61 (Rayleigh) | rayleigh | +11.6% | 0.985 | 77522 | +1.7% | 0.987 | 77522 |
| amsterdam | CHM15k A *(ref)* | rayleigh | +0.0% | 1.000 | 36437 | +0.0% | 1.000 | 36437 |
| amsterdam | CHM15k B | rayleigh | +23.2% | 0.956 | 28884 | +24.0% | 0.956 | 28884 |
| amsterdam | CHM15k C | rayleigh | +3.0% | 0.925 | 32868 | -14.9% | 0.925 | 32868 |
| amsterdam | CHM15k D | rayleigh | -7.6% | 0.931 | 34196 | -15.6% | 0.943 | 34196 |
| uccle | CL51 (cloud) *(ref)* | cloud | +0.0% | 1.000 | 66068 | +0.0% | 1.000 | 66068 |
| uccle | CL61 (cloud) | cloud | -14.3% | 0.941 | 54365 | -2.9% | 0.944 | 54365 |
| sirta | CHM15k (Rayleigh) *(ref)* | rayleigh | +0.0% | 1.000 | 193058 | +0.0% | 1.000 | 193058 |
| sirta | CL31 (cloud) | cloud | -4.0% | 0.808 | 191232 | -16.4% | 0.813 | 191232 |
| sirta | Mini-MPL (Rayleigh) | rayleigh | -54.6% | 0.794 | 88063 | -45.5% | 0.789 | 88063 |

![payerne validation](data:image/png;base64,)

![amsterdam validation](data:image/png;base64,)

![uccle validation](data:image/png;base64,)

![sirta validation](data:image/png;base64,)


## EARLINET — ceilometer (CHM15k) vs EARLINET research-lidar reference

EARLINET L2 1064 nm particle backscatter is read and converted to attenuated backscatter (assumed lidar
ratio 50 sr, molecular Rayleigh + two-way transmission, overlap hold below a site minimum;
`validation/paper/earlinet.py`), then matched to the colocated CHM15k (operational Python Rayleigh + Kalman,
calibrated over 2025-2026) within +/-30 min and compared over 500-5000 m AGL.

| site | Python relbias | Python r | matched | MATLAB relbias | MATLAB r | MATLAB N |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| ino (Magurele) | -11.8% | 0.30 | 921 | -6.8% | 0.25 | 273244 |
| ari (Leipzig)  | +43.8% | 0.16 | 1833 | +34.1% | 0.16 | 349047 |
| sir (Palaiseau)| +146.9% | 0.18 | 397 | +70.0% | 0.23 | 59252 |
| lei (Leipzig)  | no EARLINET 1064 files in the 2025-2026 calibration window | | | | | |
| cbw (Cabauw)   | no EARLINET 1064 files in the 2025-2026 calibration window | | | | | |

*EARLINET-vs-ceilometer profile-level correlation is intrinsically low (different technique, +/-30 min
sampling, assumed EARLINET lidar ratio), so r is comparable to MATLAB rather than high. **ino** and **ari**
match the MATLAB in sign, order and r; **sir** is high in both (mean relbias is outlier-sensitive). The
Python uses the 2025-2026 CHM Kalman series, the MATLAB R files span 2017-2026, so the matched samples
differ. lei/cbw have no EARLINET 1064 data inside the Python calibration window.*
